LootQualityTypes.brief: Constants defining various quality levels for loot and equipment items in the game.
LootQualityTypes.details: The LootQualityTypes group contains a set of integer constants that categorize the quality levels of loot and equipment items, such as normal, uncommon, rare, legendary, unique, and set items. These constants facilitate consistent identification, processing, and presentation of items across different systems, enabling features like rarity-based mechanics, visual differentiation, and gameplay bonuses. By standardizing item quality classification, this group supports maintainability and clarity in the game's item management logic.
LootQualityTypes.notes: The constants are organized hierarchically from common to rare and special categories, allowing easy extension or modification of quality tiers if needed.
LootQualityTypes.rationale: This group exists to provide a unified, maintainable way to reference item quality levels, ensuring consistent handling and recognition of item qualities throughout the game's codebase, which is essential for gameplay mechanics, UI display, and balancing.
LootQualityTypes.name: LootQualityTypes

EQ_QUALITY_SET.brief: A constant that signifies the 'set' quality category for equipment items within the game.
EQ_QUALITY_SET.details: The constant EQ_QUALITY_SET is used as an identifier for equipment items classified under the 'set' quality tier. Initialized to zero, it functions as a marker in various game systems, such as item generation, naming conventions, and stat modifications. When generating or modifying equipment, this constant helps determine whether an item belongs to a set, thereby influencing its attributes, appearance, and interactions. Its usage ensures consistent handling of set-quality items across different modules of the game code.
EQ_QUALITY_SET.notes: This constant is typically used in conditional checks to apply set-specific logic, such as bonus stats or special effects. It is stored as an integer value and is part of the broader LootQualityTypes enumeration, facilitating easy comparisons and extensions.
EQ_QUALITY_SET.rationale: The EQ_QUALITY_SET constant exists to standardize the identification of set-quality items, enabling the game to apply specialized behaviors, bonuses, or interactions for such items, thereby enhancing gameplay depth and consistency.
EQ_QUALITY_SET.name: EQ_QUALITY_SET

usages.lootv2_8cc, Object * generate_eq(int obj: Used as a constant to identify set-quality items, influencing vnum selection and naming.
usages.lootv2_8cc, void add_base_stats(Object *: Used to adjust armor stats by increasing min and max values by 15% when item quality matches.

EQ_QUALITY_UNIQUE.brief: Constant representing the unique quality level assigned to in-game items.
EQ_QUALITY_UNIQUE.details: The constant EQ_QUALITY_UNIQUE is used to identify items classified as unique quality within the game. Initialized to the value 1, it serves as a key identifier in functions that modify item attributes, generate new items, or assign specific properties based on quality tier. Its usage ensures consistent recognition of unique items across various systems, affecting aspects such as stat adjustments, naming conventions, and VNUM selection during item creation and management processes.
EQ_QUALITY_UNIQUE.notes: This constant is typically used in conditional checks to apply special handling or attributes to unique-quality items, ensuring they are distinguished from other quality levels in the codebase.
EQ_QUALITY_UNIQUE.rationale: The constant exists to provide a clear, maintainable way to identify and handle unique-quality items, supporting game mechanics that differentiate these items in terms of rarity, attributes, and presentation.
EQ_QUALITY_UNIQUE.name: EQ_QUALITY_UNIQUE

usages.lootv2_8cc, Object * generate_eq(int obj: Used as a constant to identify unique-quality items, influencing vnum selection and naming.
usages.lootv2_8cc, void add_base_stats(Object *: Used to adjust armor stats by increasing min and max values by 10% when item quality matches.

EQ_QUALITY_LEGENDARY.brief: Constant representing the 'Legendary' quality level for equipment items.
EQ_QUALITY_LEGENDARY.details: This constant assigns the integer value used to identify equipment items classified as 'Legendary' within the game's item quality hierarchy. It is utilized in functions responsible for generating, modifying, or evaluating equipment to ensure items of this highest quality tier are correctly labeled and styled, affecting their naming conventions, attribute bonuses, and visual effects. As part of the LootQualityTypes set, it enables consistent handling and recognition of legendary items throughout the game's systems.
EQ_QUALITY_LEGENDARY.notes: Typically stored as a fixed integer value, it is used in conditional checks and item generation routines to apply special properties or visual cues associated with legendary quality.
EQ_QUALITY_LEGENDARY.rationale: The constant exists to standardize the identification of legendary items, ensuring that functions generating or processing equipment can easily recognize and apply the appropriate attributes and effects for this top-tier quality level.
EQ_QUALITY_LEGENDARY.name: EQ_QUALITY_LEGENDARY

usages.lootv2_8cc, Object * generate_eq(int obj: Used to determine if the object should receive a legendary name and prefix modifications.
usages.lootv2_8cc, void add_base_stats(Object *: Not directly used in this function but listed as a dependency for potential quality-based adjustments.

EQ_QUALITY_RARE.brief: Constant representing the 'Rare' quality level for loot items in the game.
EQ_QUALITY_RARE.details: This constant defines the integer value associated with 'Rare' quality items within the loot quality classification system. It is used to categorize items that are considered more uncommon and potentially possess better attributes compared to lower quality levels such as 'Common' or 'Uncommon'. While it is not directly manipulated within core functions like add_base_stats, it acts as a key reference for quality-based logic, ensuring consistent handling and recognition of rare items throughout the game's systems. Its value of 3 aligns with the predefined quality hierarchy, facilitating straightforward comparisons and categorizations.
EQ_QUALITY_RARE.notes: Stored as a constexpr integer, enabling compile-time optimizations and ensuring immutability. Used as a constant reference across multiple modules to maintain consistency in quality level identification.
EQ_QUALITY_RARE.rationale: This constant exists to provide a clear, standardized identifier for 'Rare' quality items, supporting game mechanics that depend on item rarity, such as drop rates, attribute scaling, and player rewards. It helps maintain uniformity in quality classification and simplifies logic related to item rarity handling.
EQ_QUALITY_RARE.name: EQ_QUALITY_RARE

usages.lootv2_8cc, void add_base_stats(Object *: Not directly used in this function but listed as a dependency for potential quality-based adjustments.

EQ_QUALITY_UNCOMMON.brief: Defines the integer constant representing the 'Uncommon' quality level for loot items.
EQ_QUALITY_UNCOMMON.details: This constant assigns the value 4 to the 'Uncommon' quality tier within the loot quality classification system. It serves as a standardized identifier used throughout the codebase to categorize items that are of higher quality than 'Common' but below 'Rare' or 'Epic' tiers. While not directly invoked in item creation functions, it plays a crucial role in quality-based logic, such as modifying item attributes, display properties, or drop rates based on item quality. Its consistent use ensures uniform handling and easy recognition of 'Uncommon' items across different modules.
EQ_QUALITY_UNCOMMON.notes: Stored as a constexpr integer, enabling compile-time optimization and ensuring immutability. Used as a key value in quality comparison and categorization logic.
EQ_QUALITY_UNCOMMON.rationale: This constant exists to provide a clear, maintainable way to reference the 'Uncommon' quality level, facilitating quality-based item differentiation and ensuring consistent behavior across systems that process loot item qualities.
EQ_QUALITY_UNCOMMON.name: EQ_QUALITY_UNCOMMON

usages.lootv2_8cc, void add_base_stats(Object *: Not directly used in this function but listed as a dependency for potential quality-based adjustments.

EQ_QUALITY_NORMAL.brief: Defines the constant value representing the 'normal' quality level for loot items.
EQ_QUALITY_NORMAL.details: This constant, initialized to 5, signifies the standard or default quality tier assigned to loot items categorized as 'normal'. It is used throughout the codebase to identify, filter, and process loot items of this quality level, ensuring consistent handling across systems such as loot generation, item display, and quality assessments. Its usage helps standardize the representation of normal-quality loot within the game's item management framework.
EQ_QUALITY_NORMAL.notes: The constant is stored as a compile-time constant to optimize performance and ensure immutability. It serves as a key reference point for quality comparisons and categorization logic.
EQ_QUALITY_NORMAL.rationale: The constant exists to provide a clear, maintainable reference for the 'normal' loot quality level, facilitating consistent behavior and easier adjustments if the quality tier value needs to change in the future.
EQ_QUALITY_NORMAL.name: EQ_QUALITY_NORMAL
